screenshots.desktop: screenshot-desktop-qa58.png — 1280x800 captured. Vibe: Crushing It, LEGEND honorific, all 5 workers at desks. Bloberto speech bubble 'Wait you shipped at THIS hour?! Legends.' visibly BREACHES the top border of the office floor — overflow:visible confirmed again. Coffee corner 'TRIPLE SHOT' label bleeds past right edge. Sunrise window rectangle floating FREE outside floor boundary at bottom-right. Ambient particles escape the floor container. Shortcut toast visible. Whiteboard clips left edge.
screenshots.mobile: screenshot-mobile-qa58.png — 375x812 captured. DASHBOARD TAB IS INVISIBLE. Only 'Office' tab partially visible, 'Dashboard' cut off — carry-over #6 confirmed, strike 15. Speech bubble above floor top border. Vibe weather label inside floor (angled). Large dead whitespace below office. Footer renders fine. The app is functionally unusable for Dashboard access on a real phone.
screenshots.status: Both screenshots captured successfully via Puppeteer + Chromium /usr/bin/chromium, headless mode.

score_notes.visual: Dark theme is legitimately gorgeous, characters charming, vibe-reactive office is delightful. But elements visibly bleeding outside the floor border for 15 consecutive reviews cannot be ignored. Bloberto's bubble punching through the ceiling, the salmon sunrise rectangle floating free — these read as bugs to any new visitor. -0.5 for persistent boundary bleed.
score_notes.ux: Vibe streak, ping reactions, keyboard shortcuts, confetti, meeting detection — lots of good UX investment. But mobile Dashboard inaccessibility is a navigation failure. completionToast (bottom: 2rem) and plantToast (bottom: 5.5rem) can render simultaneously — not overlapping but toast soup. Maya has not shipped the fix.
score_notes.responsiveness: Office floor scales reasonably on mobile. 768px and 600px breakpoints work. But tab-toggle at 480px only adjusts margin — no overflow-x:auto, no scroll affordance, no hint that Dashboard exists. That is a navigation failure, not cosmetic. Down to 2.
score_notes.performance: Three separate visibilitychange listeners running concurrently. VIBE_LABELS literal instantiated inside useEffect every vibe change (module-scope VIBE_RANK already exists). Activity filter computed TWICE per render — once for count label (lines 1330-1342) and once for ActivityLog (lines 1387-1396). window.matchMedia called inline in JSX render on polling-paused-pill. Not catastrophic but accumulating.
score_notes.accessibility: Persistently the worst category. Five confirmed patterns of aria-live on conditionally-mounted elements: completionToast, plantToast, polling-paused-pill, vibeCaption, isFullSync/isFullIdle banners. Screen readers cannot announce these because the region does not exist when content is set. Tab panels missing tabIndex={-1}. sparkline-legend hover-only. This is strike 15. I am genuinely running out of polite words.
score_notes.code_quality: VIBE_LABELS still instantiated inside useEffect (line 1012) when VIBE_RANK already exists at module scope (line 390). Dead CSS: the single-quoted .office-floor[data-vibe='...'] block (lines 91-95) is fully superseded by the double-quoted block (lines 97-115) which sets identical values PLUS --grid-line. Lines 91-95 are dead. 16 inline style props per toast, times two toasts. Files at 1418 and 1636 lines. This is a code quality 2 and holding.
score_notes.features: Feature count is high and rising. Vibe sparkline history, task intent tagging, ghost workers, conference table meeting detection, STANDUP mode, lone survivor banners, ficus growth streak, ping reactions, keyboard shortcuts, door events, wall clock with vibe personality, activity log with time groups and filters. Rich product.
score_notes.fun_factor: Bloberto saying 'Wait you shipped at THIS hour?! Legends.' at the exact moment I take a screenshot is accidental poetry. COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT on crushing vibe. The whiteboard ASCII emoticons. The door labels. Carlos's log obsession. The personality density here is elite. The bugs almost add to the chaos energy. Almost.

carry_over_status.carlos_luna_review_83_fixes: NOT SHIPPED. Carlos is not in workers.json. No activity.json entry confirming completion. All bugs from Luna review #83 carry forward.
carry_over_status.maya_toast_overlap_fix: NOT SHIPPED. Maya is not in workers.json. plantToast (bottom:5.5rem, 16 inline props, Office.jsx:1280) and completionToast (bottom:2rem, 16 inline props, App.jsx:1142) both unchanged. 3.5rem gap prevents direct overlap but both can render simultaneously and the inline style count is unchanged.

suggestions.title: overflow:visible on .office-floor causes universal boundary bleed
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: `overflow: visible`. Confirmed in BOTH screenshots — Bloberto speech bubble above top border, coffee corner 'TRIPLE SHOT' label outside right edge, sunrise window floating free at bottom-right, ambient particles escaping floor container. Two-part fix required: (1) change .office-floor to overflow:hidden to clip the container, (2) move speech bubbles and hover cards that legitimately escape to a React portal rendered outside .office-floor. Simply setting overflow:hidden alone will clip speech bubbles which is equally wrong. The always-present <span className='sr-only' aria-live> pattern at Office.jsx:1276 shows portals are already understood — apply same approach to CharacterBubble elements.

suggestions.title: aria-live on conditionally mounted elements — 5 confirmed patterns, strike 15
suggestions.description:
Screen readers announce content changes ONLY IF the live region existed before the text changed. All five patterns mount the element simultaneously with the content: (1) completionToast: `{completionToast &&
}` App.jsx:1139, (2) plantToast: `{plantToast &&
}` Office.jsx:1277, (3) polling-paused-pill: `{pollingPaused && }` App.jsx:1187, (4) vibeCaption: `{vibeCaption &&
}` Office.jsx:1469, (5) isFullSync/isFullIdle banners Office.jsx:1310-1311. Fix: create always-present sr-only containers with aria-live, update text from state. The correct pattern already exists at Office.jsx:1276 (`{pingReaction}`) — replicate for all others. This has been called out since before anyone started counting.

suggestions.title: Dashboard tab invisible on mobile — no overflow-x handling on tab-toggle
suggestions.description: Mobile screenshot (375px) confirms: Dashboard tab is not visible. The @media (max-width: 480px) block for .tab-toggle (App.css:1137) only adjusts margin. No overflow-x:auto, no scroll affordance, no visual indicator. A real user on a phone sees only the Office tab and has no way to discover Dashboard exists. Fix: add `overflow-x: auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;` to .tab-toggle at mobile breakpoints. Add `scroll-snap-type: x mandatory` and `scroll-snap-align: start` on buttons for smooth snapping. Consider a subtle gradient fade-right to hint at scrollable content.

suggestions.title: Triple visibilitychange listeners running concurrently
suggestions.description: App.jsx has three separate document.addEventListener('visibilitychange') registrations: (1) line 852 — handleVisibilityResume (calls onResume/sync), (2) line 874 — handleVisibilityChange inside polling useEffect (adjusts poll interval), (3) line 1093 — handleVisibilityChange inside tab title useEffect (updates document.title). Listeners 1 and 2 BOTH trigger data fetches on visibility restore, causing a double fetch every time the tab becomes visible. Consolidate into a single top-level visibilitychange handler that dispatches to appropriate callbacks, or create a useDocumentVisibility hook.

suggestions.title: Inline toast styles: 16 CSS properties each on completionToast and plantToast — Maya fix not shipped
suggestions.description: completionToast (App.jsx:1142-1159) and plantToast (Office.jsx:1280-1297) each define 16 inline CSS properties: position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation. Maya was assigned this fix and has not shipped. Extract to CSS classes .completion-toast and .plant-toast. Only the dynamic border color and boxShadow (which depend on completionToast.color) need to remain as CSS custom property assignments: `style={{ '--toast-color': completionToast.color }}`. Everything else is static and belongs in a stylesheet.

suggestions.title: VIBE_LABELS and vibeRanking defined inside useEffect — duplicating module-scope VIBE_RANK
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1003: `const vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 }` — identical to VIBE_RANK at line 390. Line 1012: `const VIBE_LABELS = { 'after-hours': 'After Hours', ... }` — object literal created fresh inside a useEffect that fires every vibe change. Neither object needs to exist inside the effect. Move VIBE_LABELS to module scope. Replace vibeRanking references with the already-existing VIBE_RANK. Net result: two fewer object allocations per vibe transition and cleaner effect body.

suggestions.title: Dead duplicate CSS: single-quoted vibe selectors (lines 91-95) fully superseded by double-quoted block
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-95 use single-quoted attribute selectors ([data-vibe='crushing'] etc.) and set border-color + box-shadow. Lines 97-115 use double-quoted selectors ([data-vibe="crushing"] etc.) and set the SAME border-color + box-shadow values PLUS the missing --grid-line custom property. Both syntaxes match the same elements — the double-quoted block wins (last declaration) and fully supersedes the single-quoted block. Lines 91-95 are ~5 lines of dead CSS. Delete them. They increase parse time and confuse anyone reading the file.

suggestions.title: Activity filter logic computed twice per render — identical filter chain runs twice
suggestions.description: In App.jsx, the activity filter executes twice per render: once for the count display (IIFE inline at lines 1329-1342) and again when rendering ActivityLog (lines 1387-1396). Same activityLog array, same conditions, same render. Extract to a `useMemo` or a pre-JSX const: `const filteredActivity = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(...).filter(...), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter])`. Pass filteredActivity to both the count display and ActivityLog. Eliminates the duplicate O(n) filter pass.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia called inline in JSX render path
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1194: `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` evaluated inline inside the polling-paused-pill JSX expression. This triggers a MediaQueryList evaluation on every render of the App component (which re-renders every 60 seconds via useTimeTick, every poll, every state change). Hoist to module-level or a useState initialized once with a MediaQueryList change listener for correctness. The value changes only when a user switches from mouse to touch input, which is extremely rare.

suggestions.title: Tab panels missing tabIndex={-1} — ARIA tabpanel spec requirement
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1252 and 1256: the `role='tabpanel'` divs have no tabIndex attribute. WAI-ARIA authoring practices require tabpanel elements to have tabIndex={0} so keyboard users can Tab from the tablist into the panel content. Currently a keyboard user pressing Tab after selecting Office/Dashboard tab will skip past the panel entirely. Add `tabIndex={0}` to both tabpanel divs. Related: the tab arrow-key navigation (handleTabKeyDown) only moves between tabs, not into panels — this is correct behavior for a tablist, so the fix is only the missing tabIndex.

suggestions.title: WallClock SVG has 5 positioning props inline that belong in CSS
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1078: `style={{ position: 'absolute', left: '22%', top: '2.5%', zIndex: 1, pointerEvents: 'auto' }}` directly on the WallClock SVG element. None of these values are dynamic. The `.office-wall-clock` class already exists (used as the SVG's className). Move all 5 properties there. Inline positioning styles block responsive overrides and require `!important` in media queries to override.

suggestions.title: Lone survivor label has 8 inline style properties in Office.jsx
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1532: the .lone-survivor-label div renders with 8 inline style properties (fontSize, opacity, position, bottom, left, transform, whiteSpace, color). The class `.lone-survivor-label` is the correct home for all of these. Same pattern problem as the toasts — static visual properties that never change inline. Also: LoadingSkeleton in App.jsx has 6 skeleton divs with inline width/height/borderRadius values that should be in CSS classes.

suggestions.title: Complex IIFE for solo spotlight message is unreadable in JSX
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1285: `{(() => { const soloKey = (activeWorkers[0]?.id ?? activeWorkers[0]?.name)?.toLowerCase(); return SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES[soloKey]?.[teamVibe.key] ?? SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES[soloKey]?.default ?? '...' })()}` — a self-invoking function with 3 levels of optional chaining inline in JSX render. Extract to a helper function `getSoloSpotlightMessage(worker, vibeKey)` above the component. This is untestable as written and breaks the visual scan of the JSX tree.

suggestions.title: ConferenceTable shadows outer cx variable — same value declared 3 times in one function
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: `const cx = 52` is declared at line 890 (outer scope), then re-declared inside the Huddle block (line 969) and again inside the Standup block (line 987) — all with the same value. They shadow the outer variable without benefit. They would silently diverge if anyone changes the outer cx. Remove the inner declarations.

suggestions.title: Sparkline legend only visible on hover — inaccessible to keyboard and touch users
suggestions.description: App.css line 1154: `.stat-card:hover .sparkline-legend { display: flex }`. The vibe history legend is permanently hidden and only appears on mouse hover. Keyboard users and touch users cannot access it. Add `:focus-within` alongside `:hover`: `.stat-card:hover .sparkline-legend, .stat-card:focus-within .sparkline-legend { display: flex }`. Or make it always visible since it's 0.55rem and unobtrusive.

suggestions.title: ErrorBoundary fallback uses 6 inline style properties
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 13-15: ErrorBoundary fallback renders with `style={{ display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', justifyContent: 'center', height: '100vh', fontSize: '1.1rem', color: '#f87171' }}`. Six inline props on a crash-state UI that should have its own CSS class. Technically harmless but inconsistent with the project's approach. Extract to `.error-boundary-fallback` in App.css.

summary.carlos_fix_status: NOT SHIPPED — Carlos not active in workers.json, no activity.json entry confirming Luna review #83 bug fixes
summary.maya_fix_status: NOT SHIPPED — Maya not active in workers.json, plantToast/completionToast inline styles unchanged (16 props each, confirmed in source)
summary.score_delta: -0.3 from review #57 (3.4 -> 3.1) — zero fixes landed, six patterns at strike 15, code files growing without corresponding cleanup
summary.verdict: Zero fixes landed. Fifteen consecutive reviews with the same six bugs. The product is charming, the features are rich, the personality is genuine. But overflow:visible makes the office look broken to any new visitor. The accessibility failures mean screen reader users have been getting silence since before anyone started counting strikes. At some point 'carry-over' becomes 'will never be fixed' and the score has to reflect that. 3.1/5.
